confidence.disclosure: Route is reconstructed from al-Muqaddasi's own Ahsan al-Taqasim, but he gives few firm dates outside a handful of episodes (965-974 Aleppo, 978 Hajj, 984 Khurasan, 985 Shiraz). Most of the stops below are securely attested as visited but their precise years are inferred from Basil Collins' translation and André Miquel's reconstruction; precision is therefore mostly 'period'.

sources: Al-Muqaddasi, Ahsan al-Taqasim fi Ma'rifat al-Aqalim (985)
Basil Anthony Collins (trans.), The Best Divisions for Knowledge of the Regions (1994)
André Miquel, La géographie humaine du monde musulman jusqu'au milieu du 11e siècle, vol. 1 (1967)

journey.event: Born in Jerusalem (Bayt al-Maqdis) into a family of architects; his grandfather had built the maritime defences of Acre for Ibn Tulun.
Performed his first Hajj at the age of about twenty, after which he resolved to devote himself to geography.
Visited Medina on his return from the Hajj, recording the city in his section on the Arabian peninsula.
Visited Aleppo sometime between 965 and 974, leaving a famous list of the city's gates.
Recorded Damascus as one of the principal cities of his native province of al-Sham.
Described Tiberias, capital of the district of Jordan in his administrative scheme.
Recorded Ramla, the administrative capital of Palestine district.
Visited the newly founded Fatimid capital and the older centre of Fustat.
Recorded Alexandria as the chief Mediterranean port of Egypt.
Reached the Maghrib, recording the great mosque-city of Qayrawan.
Visited the Abbasid capital, which he ranked alongside Damascus and Fustat.
Recorded Basra as the principal port of southern Iraq.
Performed his second Hajj.
Visited the Indian Ocean port of Aden as part of his survey of the Arabian peninsula.
Recorded Tabriz in his account of Azerbaijan and the northern Iranian provinces.
Visited Rayy, the great Buyid metropolis south of modern Tehran.
Travelled through Khurasan, recording Nishapur in detail.
Reached Samanid Bukhara on his Khurasan-Transoxiana circuit.
Visited Samarkand, completing his survey of Transoxiana.
Settled in Shiraz, where he composed Ahsan al-Taqasim and recorded his famous exchange with Persian stonecutters.
